name: arc-transform-tithe-revolt: transformArcSetup canonicalizes raw tool input into a Sf2ArcPlan

source.purpose: Pure-function check on lib/sf2/arc-author/transform.ts. Verifies snake_case → camelCase mapping, scenarioShape mode passthrough, durableForces / durableNpcSeeds / arcThreads / latentArcQuestions / playerStanceAxes parsing, and that chapterFunctionMap holds exactly 5 chapters. Uses a Tithe-revolt-shaped raw tool input.

arcTransform.seedHookPremise: A frontier settlement is short on its quarterly Resonant tithe to the Synod.
arcTransform.seedHookCrucible: Force the PC to choose between Synod compliance and settlement survival.

rawToolInput.title: The Tithe (Revolt)

source_hook.title: The Tithe
source_hook.premise: A frontier settlement is short on its quarterly Resonant tithe to the Synod.
source_hook.objective: Resolve the shortfall before the Synod escalates.
source_hook.crucible: Force the PC to choose between Synod compliance and settlement survival.
source_hook.first_episode: Warden arrives to find the settlement in open dispute over the tithe roster.

scenario_shape.premise: The shortage has become a public legitimacy crisis before the PC arrives.
scenario_shape.why_this_run: The settlement has already organized; arrival lands inside contested authority.
scenario_shape.what_this_is_not: Not a quiet investigation; not a chamber-play hearing.
scenario_shape.selection_rationale: Revolt was chosen over investigation because the shortage is already public; chosen over pursuit because the missing tithe has not yet moved.
scenario_shape.rejected_default_shape: Default-collapse to a hearing-room audit was rejected; the dispute is in the streets, not behind a desk.

rawToolInput.arc_question: Will the PC's authority survive a public legitimacy crisis without breaking either the Synod or the settlement?
rawToolInput.core_crucible: Force the PC to choose between Synod compliance and settlement survival.
invariant_facts: The settlement is short by three Resonants.
The Synod has dispatched the Warden as enforcer.
variable_truths_for_this_run: The shortage is publicly known, not concealed.
A faction in the settlement is openly refusing replacement selection.

durable_npc_seeds.private_pressure: Knows replacement roster will name her cousin

arc_threads.title: Synod compliance pressure
arc_threads.question: Will compliance protect or consume the settlement?
arc_threads.resolution_criteria: The PC creates a binding answer to the tithe shortfall.
arc_threads.failure_mode: The Synod turns the settlement into an example.

arc_threads.title: Public refusal momentum
arc_threads.question: Can refusal become leverage without becoming rupture?
arc_threads.resolution_criteria: The settlement accepts a concrete next step with named costs.
arc_threads.failure_mode: The council steps become the real court.

arc_threads.title: Undrift extraction temptation
arc_threads.question: Does the hidden route save people or break the settlement?
arc_threads.resolution_criteria: The PC exposes, protects, or redirects the extraction route.
arc_threads.failure_mode: The hidden channel becomes proof of betrayal.
arc_threads.retrieval_cue: Undrift route, missing rooms, hidden pressure

latent_arc_questions.question: Who made the shortage public before the Warden arrived?
latent_arc_questions.why_it_matters: The answer could re-read refusal as courage or manipulation.
latent_arc_questions.answer_impact_axis: whose legitimacy and culpability the settlement re-reads

chapter_function_map.function: Establish the public refusal and the Warden's footing
chapter_function_map.pressure_question: Can the PC enter the dispute without picking a side prematurely?
possible_end_states: PC has a thread to pull
PC is publicly committed to one pole

chapter_function_map.pressure_question: Does the PC tolerate the hidden route?
What stands when the dust settles?

possible_endgames: Synod compliance preserved at the cost of settlement legitimacy
Settlement preserved at the cost of Synod standing
PC absorbed both costs into a private bargain
